name: Gravitational waves propagate at the speed of light
requirement: Any candidate framework must predict that gravitational-wave propagation speed c_gw equals the speed of light c to within the bound set by GW170817 and its electromagnetic counterpart GRB 170817A. The gravitational and gamma-ray signals arrived within ~1.7 seconds of each other after travelling ~40 Mpc, giving |c_gw - c|/c at the level of ~10^-15.

regime.description: Cosmological propagation distance (~40 Mpc) of GW frequencies in the ~10-1000 Hz LIGO band.

current_bound.reference: Abbott et al., 'Gravitational Waves and Gamma-Rays from a Binary Neutron Star Merger: GW170817 and GRB 170817A', ApJL 848, L13 (2017), arXiv:1710.05834

what_it_excludes: This single observation killed most viable scalar-tensor and vector-tensor modifications of gravity that had been proposed to explain dark energy: large classes of Galileon, beyond-Horndeski, TeVeS, and Einstein-Aether models with non-trivial conformal/disformal couplings predicted c_gw not equal to c at observable levels. The GW170817 bound effectively closed those branches of modified gravity overnight.

parameterization.name: Direct: fractional speed deviation delta_g = (c_gw - c)/c. In Horndeski/beyond-Horndeski scalar-tensor, equivalently c_T^2 - 1.
parameterization.summary: A single scalar parameter. Any framework that propagates tensor (spin-2) GW perturbations on a generic background metric has a calculable c_gw.

procedural.predicted_observable: c_gw is the propagation speed of transverse-traceless metric perturbations h_ij in the framework's effective tensor-mode equation. For Horndeski-class theories: c_T^2 = 1 + (Higher-order kinetic and disformal terms)/G_4, with explicit formulas in Ezquiaga-Zumalacarregui.
framework_inputs: Equation of motion for tensor perturbations h_ij^TT around a cosmological FLRW or generic background.
From that EOM, read off the coefficient of the Laplacian (sets c_gw^2) and the coefficient of d^2/dt^2 (sets normalization). Their ratio is c_gw^2.
procedural.notes: This is the cleanest single-line constraint in modern fundamental physics. Matching the framework to the bound is a one-line algebraic check.

primary_references.citation: Abbott et al. (LVC + Fermi-GBM + INTEGRAL), 'Gravitational Waves and Gamma-Rays from a Binary Neutron Star Merger: GW170817 and GRB 170817A', ApJL 848, L13 (2017)
Ezquiaga and Zumalacarregui, 'Dark Energy After GW170817: Dead Ends and the Road Ahead', PRL 119, 251304 (2017)
Creminelli and Vernizzi, 'Dark Energy after GW170817 and GRB170817A', PRL 119, 251302 (2017)
